channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Project Helix Is Xbox’s Next Console Codename
item.content:encoded:
Xbox has announced the project name of its next-generation console.

Today, Xbox announced that its next-generation console is named Project Helix. New Xbox CEO Asha Sharma promises that the next console will lead in performance and serve as a hybrid for Xbox and PC games. Sharma says she will be speaking more about this with partners and game studios at her first GDC next week.

Asha Sharma Helms Xbox’s Next Steps Into the Future

Asha Sharma was put into power in the final week of February 2026. She has consistently expressed her desire to recommit to core Xbox players. She believes that will be accomplished through games with deep emotional resonance.

AMD recently said that it is on track to support a 2027 launch for new Xbox hardware. However, Microsoft isn’t interested in committing to a hard launch date at this time.

Recent memory shortages have cast doubt on when the next generation of console gaming will truly begin. Reportedly, the PlayStation 6 may not even release until 2029. Learn more about that here.

Xbox’s Project Helix May Face Opposition from Steam Machine

Still, Xbox remains at a crossroads. They’re far removed from the days of the Xbox 360’s dominance and have even pivoted more towards the Sega route of being a high-profile third-party publisher, releasing games on Sony and Nintendo platforms as well as on their own.

Asha Sharma recently responded to a fan on social media, letting them know that she heard their plea for a return to Xbox-exclusive titles. While that may be a step too far in reverse, Xbox will now look to lead the market in PC-console hybrids.

They will face competition from Valve’s forthcoming Steam Machine. That particular hybrid hardware is slated for a release in this calendar year. Learn the latest on the Steam Machine here.

In any case, Project Helix has been announced, and Insider Gaming will continue to report any updates on future hardware releases.

Are you excited for the next generation of Xbox hardware? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

item.title: Today’s Wordle #1721: Hints and Answer for March 6, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Today’s tips and strategies can help you solve it faster and maintain your streak—if you’re open to a little extra help. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Wordle, don’t worry, below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answer for Wordle Game #1721 for March 6, 2026

To play today’s Wordle, head to the game’s website. If you want to go right to today’s answer without any tips or tricks, you can click right here.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s and yesterday’s editions of Wordle. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Wordle

Wordle is a simple but addictive word puzzle. Each day, players have to guess a five-letter word answer in no more than six attempts. After each guess:

  • Green letters are correct and in the right position.
  • Yellow letters are correct, but in the wrong position.
  • Gray letters are not in the word at all.

The goal is to guess the word in as few tries as possible. Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Wordle can only be played on the New York Times website. There is currently no app for you to download the official game.

Wordle Hints For Today, March 6, 2026

The Wordle answer for game #1721 will give a slight challenge to many. According to the NY Times Wordle review, today’s answer takes an average of 4.5 out of 6 guesses. That makes it a half-guess easier than the 5-guess average from March 5.

General Wordle Hints For March 6

Without giving today’s Wordle answer completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: This word is usually used as a casual description, not formal language.
  • Hint 2: It often refers to something that feels messy or unpleasant.
  • Hint 3: The thing being described is typically hard to clean or deal with.
  • Hint 4: You’re more likely to hear this word in everyday conversation than in professional writing.

Use these clues to narrow down your guesses and increase your chances of solving Wordle #1721 for March 6, 2026.

Starter Words For March 6, 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Wordle answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you figure out some included letters. A few starter words that may help are:

  • GLIDE
  • CHUNK
  • GUSTY
  • FUNKY

Today’s Wordle Answer For Game #1721 On March 6, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answer to today’s Wordle below.

Answer: GUNKY

If you solved today’s Wordle without our answer, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

What Does Today’s Wordle Answer Mean?

GUNKY is an adjective that refers to anything that has “sticky or greasy residue or accumulation.”

Yesterday’s Wordle Answer

If you missed the previous game, here’s yesterday’s Wordle answer for Game #1720 on March 5, 2026

Answer: SHEEP

While not always the case, looking at previous words can sometimes give you insight into common patterns or letter usage in Wordle puzzles.

When Is The Next Wordle Game After #1721?

Wordle games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours before to give players a heads up. The game on March 7 will be Wordle Game #1722.

What Is Wordle?

Wordle is a daily word puzzle created by Josh Wardle. Initially developed as a prototype in 2013, it wasn’t fully released until 2021. The game challenges players to guess a five-letter word in six tries or fewer, using hints from each attempt to narrow down the answer. Its straightforward concept, accessibility, and the thrill of solving a new puzzle each day helped it surge in popularity by late 2021. The success of Wordle inspired numerous spin-offs and social media challenges, leading to its acquisition by The New York Times in early 2022.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Marathon Season 1 Rewards Pass—How To Earn Silk & All Rewards, Explained
item.content:encoded:
Marathon has finally arrived, and just like every other live service title, you bet your bottom dollar that there’s a Season Pass at launch.

In Marathon, it’s called a Rewards Pass, and the first one has arrived alongside the game’s launch. It features over 50 items for you to unlock, but you’ll need to earn a special currency: Silk. However, it’s not entirely obvious how you earn it, and you won’t want to miss it, as the Rewards Pass has some nifty cosmetics up for grabs.

If you want to make a start on Marathon’s Season 1 Rewards Pass, then you’re in the right place. This guide will explain how you can earn Silk, whilst also showcasing all of the rewards on offer.

How To Earn Silk In Marathon

In order to earn Silk in Marathon, you’ll need to level up your Season Level, which can be seen in the top left of the home screen.

You’ll increase your Season Level by heading out on Runs and completing Contracts for the various in-game Factions. Unfortunately, you can’t see the reward path for the Season, but believe in the fact that you’ll earn bundles of Silk with every level-up, with level 100 being the cap.

  • More Insider Gaming: Every Runner Shell In Marathon, Explained

However, once you’ve earned some Silk, make sure you spend it fairly quickly, as there’s a limit to how much you can store. You can only have up to 140 Silk to your name at once, so once you’ve earned enough to unlock the next reward in the Pass, it’s best you do so sooner rather than later.

Every Item In Marathon’s Season 1 Rewards Pass

There are technically 52 items to unlock in Marathon’s Season 1 Rewards Pass, though most of the rewards are bundled into pairs: one for the regular Rewards Pass, and one for the Premium.

Thankfully, these rewards are purely cosmetic, so don’t feel like you need to buy the Premium to gain an advantage. That being said, some awesome skins are behind the paywall, so if you’re into rocking the best fits in the game, you might be interested.

  • More Insider Gaming: Marathon 2026 Roadmap

The full list of rewards is available in the table below, split between the Base and Premium tiers:

What do you make of Marathon’s first Rewards Pass? Anything in there that you fancy? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Marathon content, check out how the Seasonal Wipes work, as well as when Ranked is joining the game.

MOBILE APP
